steps.title: Find the slope from the two known data points
steps.content:
The population increased from 57 thousand in 1991 to 224 thousand in 2011. The change is $224 - 57 = 167$ thousand over $2011 - 1991 = 20$ years. The slope is:

$$m = \frac{167}{20} = 8.35 \text{ thousand per year}$$

steps.title: Project to 2015 using the linear model
steps.content: From 1991 to 2015 is $2015 - 1991 = 24$ years. With a constant rate of 8.35 thousand per year, the population grows by: $$8.35 \times 24 = 200.4 \text{ thousand}$$ Starting from 57 thousand in 1991: $$x = 57 + 200.4 = 257.4$$ Rounded to the nearest whole number, the value of $x$ is 257.
